summary: Implement a new user profile page to display user information using shadcn/ui components and integrate it into the application's routing.

steps.description: Create the User Profile Page component. This page will display user details such as name, email, and a profile picture. It will utilize shadcn/ui components like Card, Avatar, Label, Input, Button, and Separator for a clean and consistent UI.
steps.details: Create a new file `ProfilePage.tsx` in `client/src/pages/`. This component will define the layout and content for the user profile. It will include a main card to hold the profile information, an avatar for the user's picture, and input fields (disabled for display) for user data. A 'Edit Profile' button will be included as a placeholder for future functionality.

steps.description: Integrate the new ProfilePage into the application's routing. This involves adding a new route definition to `client/src/App.tsx` so that the profile page can be accessed via a specific URL path.
steps.details: Modify `client/src/App.tsx` to import `ProfilePage.tsx` and add a new `<Route>` component. The path for this route will be `/profile`, making the user profile accessible at `http://yourwebsite.com/profile`. This assumes `react-router-dom` is already set up in `App.tsx`.

steps.description: Verify the new user profile page is accessible and displays correctly. This involves navigating to the new route and checking the UI.
steps.details: Start the development server. Open the browser and navigate to `/profile`. Confirm that the `ProfilePage` component renders, displays the placeholder user information, and all shadcn/ui components are styled correctly. Check for any console errors.

risks: Potential styling conflicts if global CSS rules interfere with shadcn/ui components.
Incorrect routing setup in `App.tsx` could prevent the page from loading.
rollback_strategy: Revert the changes in `client/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx` and `client/src/App.tsx` to their previous versions. This will remove the new page and its route, restoring the application to its state before the feature implementation.
success_criteria: The user can navigate to `/profile` and see a well-formatted user profile page displaying placeholder user information using shadcn/ui components without any console errors.
original_request: create a page for displaying the user profile
